title: machaeranthera canescens var. shastensis
intro: Machaeranthera canescens var. shastensis, also known as Shasta false goldenrod, is a perennial herb native to the western United States. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Machaeranthera canescens var. shastensis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and provide relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, salves, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
